item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept and tests, but its a bit drafty as is. As well, clinical tone is a bit inconsistent and the text blocks can be hard to read.

Tl; DR Great concept, but needs some editing. You can PM me if you'd like to arrange a date to touch-up the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Emotional support bread is the best kind of bread.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it. Well written, unique and wholesome SCPs are rare and a sentient, kindly bread bag is great. That's a +1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the event this is in response to my comment, I totally get it. The Foundation needs D-Class, im well aware they are necessary. I was just bringing up the idea that the researchers might also technicly be "bad" people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, they're doing it because if they don't the world would have been destroyed millenia ago.

And besides, all D-Class are on death row anyways, so borrowing them to help test and keep things that would kill us all in check wouldn't be too bad in the Bread's eyes.

Think about it; the Foundation has to deal with Sarkic warlords, extradimensional gods, an omnicidal lizard that's also an extradimensional god, (leviathan of the fourth bride), a statue that will snap their neck if they dont give it visual attention, The Scarlet bloody King trying to break free and reign hell on them, Wondertainment, The Factory, etc. spewing out more SCPs that they have to deal with, making sure the Broken God doesn't reform and reshape the universe, chaos itself, Uriel who can end the Earth is he dare so moves, a little girl that has no control over her power to literally do anything she wants, a machine that will literally make the universe inhabitable if activated, and the knowledge that they are simply fiction being orchestrated at the hands of the true God.

What's a few criminals who are going to die anyways, to all of that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got the impression that the bag leans heavily towards re-education before punishment, only drawing the line at people who are utterly unsalvagable - many researchers are not too concerned about ethics, but I imagine that all but the very worst would be seen as just needing a small nudge in the right direction to become more empathic people.

Though it might result in researchers that are unable to work with human test subjects when testing dangerous SCPs due to strong moral backbones that disallows them from sacrificing others for the greater good, and forces them to be reassigned to positions where human or cruel testing is not relevant.

Something that might be worth exploring is that they still know of inhumane treatment of many people however, and may even form some kind of political coalition in order to put a stop on the most cruel, callous or unnecessary mistreatments within the SCP Foundation. No doubt some researchers will notice that most supporters of the humane policy changes have taken part of SCP-3764 and add new containment procedures to control exposure to its "ethical" healing so that they don't make the ethics coalition too strong (fuck, is this how the ethics committee came to be? Or at least how they gained more power within The Foundation to curb the worst excesses?) and don't lose difficult-to-replace researchers that have to do some unsavory stuff to keep humanity safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would agree that Foundation researchers are pretty terrible, but the bread only interacts with them once, and that's to tell them to stop being dicks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I like this a lot, but here's a small thing that I don't get.

The Bread is nice to most people unless you have committed some inhuman monstrous act.
(As told in the last entry)
But it has acted casual and nice with foundation researchers.
Researchers who expend D-class like tissue paper.
Surely The Bread feels this is an inhuman act aswell.
Even if the researchers pay the D-class with respect, they are still activly sending people to their death without batting an eye. After all, such is their purpose.
Unless the researchers assigned are new of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't really a fan of the final experiment; I feel like "it's nice unless you're an awful person" wasn't entirely necessary. That being said, the D-Class sharing their bread and being rewarded for it was so nice and made me smile so much that you could have written almost whatever you wanted and I'd still have upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…welp, I just posted a review of this in the forums: http://scp-wiki.net/forum/t-6147790/small-miracles

As such, I personally wasn't a huge fan, but I did like certain bits of it. I'm glad that the Thaumiel/Safe issue was addressed, but it's not quite there for an upvote for me here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That last one reminded me - bread is the staff of life, and you can hit someone with a staff.

I like the general wholesomeness of this. That I routinely bake bread for home consumption may play into that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Vengeful Bread Friend.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting SCP. Also, the end makes me wondering if this SCP is sentient enough that it would help those who need it and unleash the wrath to those who had done atrocious acts.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a wholesome bag of bread. Simple idea, but executed pretty darn well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of all the things the Foundation has to deal with.

We have demons about to destroy the world, realities that tear you apart, things we can't even explain, humanity turning against us, skinned cats, evil surveys, all while the O5 Command fears death, while we learn that we could be erased at any moment, and figure out our life is a lie.

We have something to calm all of that fear, and with some bread because why not bread?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find it quite charming. It's like a weak SCP-500 , but really gentle and generous. It's no miraculous medicine, yet heals the soul.

Nice work!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My personal headcanon for D-Class is a little different, and also the bread is so plentiful that distributing it like a medicine would not be a drain on Foundation resources.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Testing log, fifth entry reads as "Cell Clock K". Assuming that's a typo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Small issue

Requested permission for use of SCP-3764-1 as a sleep aid, which was granted.

Would a D-Class really be given free usage of an SCP?
